title: Brachiaria whiteana

More specific classification information may be found in botanical resources or databases.

intro: Brachiaria whiteana, also known as White's signal grass, is a tropical grass species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Brachiaria whiteana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
